heading: Federal authority over intrastate transportation
label: 14501 Federal authority over intrastate transportation
notes: References in TextThe Surface Freight Forwarder Deregulation Act of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is , , . L. 114–942005—Subsec. (c)(2)(B). , inserted “intrastate” before “transportation”.Pub. L. 109–59, § 4206(a)Subsec. (c)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 109–59, § 4105(a)2002—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 107–2981998—Subsec. (a). reenacted heading without change and amended text of subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “No State or political subdivision thereof and no interstate agency or other political agency of 2 or more States shall enact or enforce any law, rule, regulation, standard, or other provision having the force and effect of law relating to scheduling of interstate or intrastate transportation (including discontinuance or reduction in the level of service) provided by motor carrier of passengers subject to jurisdiction under subchapter I of chapter 135 of this title on an interstate route or relating to the implementation of any change in the rates for such transportation or for any charter transportation except to the extent that notice, not in excess of 30 days, of changes in schedules may be required. This subsection shall not apply to intrastate commuter bus operations.”Pub. L. 105–178Subsec. (a)(1). substituted “operations, or to intrastate bus transportation of any nature in the State of Hawaii” for “operations” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 105–277

Effective Date of 2015 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under , Government Organization and Employees.Pub. L. 114–94section 1003 of Pub. L. 114–94section 5313 of Title 5Oct. 1, 2015

children.label: A
children.text: scheduling of interstate or intrastate transportation (including discontinuance or reduction in the level of service) provided by a motor carrier of passengers subject to jurisdiction under subchapter I of chapter 135 of this title on an interstate route;

children.label: B
children.text: the implementation of any change in the rates for such transportation or for any charter transportation except to the extent that notice, not in excess of 30 days, of changes in schedules may be required; or

children.label: C
children.text: the authority to provide intrastate or interstate charter bus transportation.

children.heading: .—Matters not covered
children.label: 2 .—Matters not covered
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not restrict the safety regulatory authority of a State with respect to motor vehicles, the authority of a State to impose highway route controls or limitations based on the size or weight of the motor vehicle, or the authority of a State to regulate carriers with regard to minimum amounts of financial responsibility relating to insurance requirements and self-insurance authorization.

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: 1 .—General rule
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2) of this subsection, no State or political subdivision thereof and no intrastate agency or other political agency of 2 or more States shall enact or enforce any law, rule, regulation, standard, or other provision having the force and effect of law relating to intrastate rates, intrastate routes, or intrastate services of any freight forwarder or broker.

children.heading: .—Continuation of hawaii’s authority
children.label: 2 .—Continuation of hawaii’s authority
children.text: Nothing in this subsection and the amendments made by the Surface Freight Forwarder Deregulation Act of 1986 shall be construed to affect the authority of the State of Hawaii to continue to regulate a motor carrier operating within the State of Hawaii.

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: 1 .—General rule
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), a State, political subdivision of a State, or political authority of 2 or more States may not enact or enforce a law, regulation, or other provision having the force and effect of law related to a price, route, or service of any motor carrier (other than a carrier affiliated with a direct air carrier covered by section 41713(b)(4)) or any motor private carrier, broker, or freight forwarder with respect to the transportation of property.

children.label: A
children.text: shall not restrict the safety regulatory authority of a State with respect to motor vehicles, the authority of a State to impose highway route controls or limitations based on the size or weight of the motor vehicle or the hazardous nature of the cargo, or the authority of a State to regulate motor carriers with regard to minimum amounts of financial responsibility relating to insurance requirements and self-insurance authorization;

children.label: B
children.text: does not apply to the intrastate transportation of household goods; and

children.label: C
children.text: does not apply to the authority of a State or a political subdivision of a State to enact or enforce a law, regulation, or other provision relating to the regulation of tow truck operations performed without the prior consent or authorization of the owner or operator of the motor vehicle.

children.label: i
children.text: uniform cargo liability rules,

children.label: ii
children.text: uniform bills of lading or receipts for property being transported,

children.label: iii
children.text: uniform cargo credit rules,

children.label: iv
children.text: antitrust immunity for joint line rates or routes, classifications, mileage guides, and pooling, or

children.label: v
children.text: antitrust immunity for agent-van line operations (as set forth in section 13907),

children.label: i
children.text: the law, regulation, or provision covers the same subject matter as, and compliance with such law, regulation, or provision is no more burdensome than compliance with, a provision of this part or a regulation issued by the Secretary or the Board under this part; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the law, regulation, or provision only applies to a carrier upon request of such carrier.

children.heading: .—Election
children.label: C .—Election
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a carrier affiliated with a direct air carrier through common controlling ownership may elect to be subject to a law, regulation, or provision of a State, political subdivision, or political authority under this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Nonapplicability to hawaii
children.label: 4 .—Nonapplicability to hawaii
children.text: This subsection shall not apply with respect to the State of Hawaii.

children.heading: .—Limitation on statutory construction
children.label: 5 .—Limitation on statutory construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prevent a State from requiring that, in the case of a motor vehicle to be towed from private property without the consent of the owner or operator of the vehicle, the person towing the vehicle have prior written authorization from the property owner or lessee (or an employee or agent thereof) or that such owner or lessee (or an employee or agent thereof) be present at the time the vehicle is towed from the property, or both.

children.label: A
children.text: meets all applicable registration requirements under chapter 139 for the interstate transportation of passengers;

children.label: B
children.text: meets all applicable vehicle and intrastate passenger licensing requirements of the State or States in which the motor carrier is domiciled or registered to do business; and

children.label: i
children.text: transportation by the motor carrier from one State, including intermediate stops, to a destination in another State; or

children.label: ii
children.text: transportation by the motor carrier from one State, including intermediate stops in another State, to a destination in the original State.

children.heading: .—Intermediate stop defined
children.label: 2 .—Intermediate stop defined
children.text: In this section, the term “intermediate stop”, with respect to transportation by a motor carrier, means a pause in the transportation in order for one or more passengers to engage in personal or business activity, but only if the driver providing the transportation to such passenger or passengers does not, before resuming the transportation of such passenger (or at least 1 of such passengers), provide transportation to any other person not included among the passengers being transported when the pause began.

children.label: A
children.text: as subjecting taxicab service to regulation under chapter 135 or section 31138;

children.label: B
children.text: as prohibiting or restricting an airport, train, or bus terminal operator from contracting to provide preferential access or facilities to one or more providers of pre-arranged ground transportation service; and

children.label: C
children.text: as restricting the right of any State or political subdivision of a State to require, in a nondiscriminatory manner, that any individual operating a vehicle providing prearranged ground transportation service originating in the State or political subdivision have submitted to pre-licensing drug testing or a criminal background investigation of the records of the State in which the operator is domiciled, by the State or political subdivision by which the operator is licensed to provide such service, or by the motor carrier providing such service, as a condition of providing such service.
